messages.content:
HI, can you please improve the following: The monthly cost of insurance of this IA policy is $6,796.93. Based on the current cash value $447,136.26, the policy will remain in force for another 65months. This number could change in the future depending on the investment performance of the policy, but as is right now it is projected to last another 65months. 

$250,000 annual premium is probably the original premium that was set at the time of placing the policy back in 2002. And, IA’s system issues this notice automatically each year on the policy anniversary. I can’t find out at this very moment what the original plan was but since inception Bob made a total deposit of $1,695,613.75, and haven’t made any further deposits for many years.

I did not want the notice to be sent out to Bob, but our team beat me to it and sent it without my approval…Hope Bob wasn’t too upset about this notice. We also asked IA to stop sending out the notice going forward. We monitor the policy quarterly to make sure the policy remains in force. The policy currently has over $850,000 of deposit room if Bob would like to deposit more into the policy (which I doubt he would..).

The monthly cost of insurance for the IA policy is currently $6,796.93. With a current cash value of $447,136.26, the policy is projected to remain in force for another 65 months, though this projection may change based on the policy's investment performance in the future.

The $250,000 annual premium was likely the original premium set when the policy was established in 2002. Our system automatically issues this notice on the policy anniversary each year. While I don't have immediate access to the original plan details, since the policy's inception, Bob has made a total deposit of $1,695,613.75. It's important to note that he has not made any additional deposits in recent years.

While I intended to prevent the notice from being sent to Bob, our team sent it before I could intervene. I hope Bob wasn't too upset about receiving it. Moving forward, we have instructed IA to refrain from sending similar notices. We regularly monitor the policy on a quarterly basis to ensure its continued effectiveness. Furthermore, there is currently a deposit room of over $850,000 available in the policy, although it seems unlikely that Bob would choose to make any further deposits.

I’m not sure when you and Debra are leaving for vacation but I’d suggest we first process the fund switch from Dividend Growth to High Interest savings then withdraw around October 12th. If this is okay with you, we will send the switch forms right away, then send another DocuSign request for withdrawal later in October. DocuSign can be completed anywhere as long as you have access to your email. 

As for the premium, you could send us a post dated cheque (to Oct 19) made payable to Manulife. Or, you could also make an online payment to Manulife, whichever is convenient for you.

I'm uncertain of the specific dates for your vacation, but I would recommend processing the fund switch from Dividend Growth to High Interest savings before withdrawing around October 12th. If this timeline works for you, we can proceed by sending the necessary switch forms immediately and then follow up with a DocuSign request for the withdrawal in October. The DocuSign process can be completed from anywhere as long as you have access to your email.

Regarding the premium payment, you have the option of sending us a post-dated check payable to Manulife (to October 19), or you can make an online payment directly to Manulife, whichever is more convenient for you.

We received a notification from Industrial Alliance that you are looking to transfer your RRSP and LIF to Credential Securities. I wanted to check in with you and confirm if this is your intention. If so, is there any reason you want to transfer-out your RRSP and LIF at Industrial Alliance? 
I need to highlight that your Industrial Alliance RRSP and LIF investment offers advantages like capital protection. At contract maturity or death, this protection allows you to recover of the amounts invested if the market value of the funds is lower. When the market value is higher than the death benefit guarantee, you can lock in the gains once every year (till your 80th birthday) to protect your LIF investment funds during market fluctuation. In addition, your LIF contracts at Industrial Alliance make it possible to designate a beneficiary and therefore avoid paying several fees associated with estate settlement, including probate fees. It also offers creditor protection to limit your risk of loss in case of lawsuits. These are considerable advantages that other institutions might not be able to provide.

We recently received a notification from Industrial Alliance stating that you are interested in transferring your RRSP and LIF to Credential Securities. I wanted to reach out and confirm if this is indeed your intention. If it is, I would like to understand the reasons behind your decision to transfer-out your RRSP and LIF from Industrial Alliance.

It's important to highlight that your Industrial Alliance RRSP and LIF investments offer certain advantages, such as capital protection. This means that at contract maturity or in the event of your death, you have the ability to recover the amounts invested even if the market value of the funds is lower. Additionally, if the market value exceeds the death benefit guarantee, you can lock in the gains once every year (until your 80th birthday) to safeguard your LIF investment funds during market fluctuations.

Another advantage of your LIF contracts with Industrial Alliance is the ability to designate a beneficiary, which helps to avoid several fees associated with estate settlement, including probate fees. Furthermore, these contracts provide creditor protection, reducing your risk of loss in the case of lawsuits. These benefits are significant and may not be available or provided to the same extent by other institutions.

Please consider these advantages before finalizing your decision to transfer your RRSP and LIF. Let me know if you have any further questions or if there is anything else I can assist you with.

We recently received a notification from Industrial Alliance stating that you are interested in transferring your RRSP and LIF to Credential Securities. Further to my voice message, I wanted to reach out and confirm if this is indeed your intention. I also wanted to highlight that your Industrial Alliance RRSP and LIF investments offer certain advantages such as principal guarantee at investment maturity or at your passing, by passing the probate and creditor protection that might be offered by the other institution. These advantages can protect your investment and mitigate the risks against the market downtown. Bypassing the probate could also provide a much smoother estate administration. While we support your decision, please let us know if you would like to have a call with us to discuss further.

We recently received a notification from Industrial Alliance stating that you are interested in transferring your RRSP and LIF to Credential Securities. I wanted to follow up and confirm if this is indeed your intention. 

I also wanted to highlight the advantages that your Industrial Alliance RRSP and LIF investments offer. These advantages include a principal guarantee at investment maturity or in the event of your passing, along with the ability to bypass probate and creditor protection. These features can serve to protect your investments and mitigate risks associated with market downturns. Additionally, bypassing probate can provide a smoother estate administration process.

While we fully support your decision, we also want to offer the opportunity to have a call with us to discuss further if you would like. Please let us know if you would like to schedule a call to address any questions or concerns you may have.
please add sentence that these advantages might not be provided by the other institution.

We recently received a notification from Industrial Alliance stating that you are interested in transferring your RRSP and LIF to Credential Securities. I wanted to follow up and confirm if this is indeed your intention. 

I also wanted to highlight the advantages that your Industrial Alliance RRSP and LIF investments offer. These advantages include a principal guarantee at investment maturity or in the event of your passing, along with the ability to bypass probate and creditor protection. These features can serve to protect your investments and mitigate risks associated with market downturns. It's important to note that these advantages might not be provided by the other institution you are considering transferring to.

While we fully support your decision, we also want to offer the opportunity to have a call with us to discuss further if you would like. Please let us know if you would like to schedule a call to address any questions or concerns you may have.

I believe Violet already advised you while you were in our office a couple of weeks ago that we would wait for Nuvero’s confirmation before we act on the signed forms for IA and Manulife Bank. After further discussion with Nuvero, it is decided that we hold off on the process of changing the owner of these accounts to the family trust. Please note that the signed forms will be destroyed for compliance reasons. Please do not hesitate to reach out directly to Nuvero if you have any questions or concerns.

I apologize for any confusion or miscommunication. It was my understanding that we would wait for confirmation from Nuvero before proceeding with the signed forms for Industrial Alliance and Manulife Bank to change the owner of these accounts to the family trust. However, upon further discussion with Nuvero, it has been decided that we should hold off on this process for now.

Please be aware that the signed forms will be destroyed for compliance reasons. If you have any questions or concerns, I encourage you to reach out directly to Nuvero. They will be able to provide you with further information and address any inquiries you may have.
please rephrase the first sentence. We already let the person know that we will wait for Nuvero to confirm before we process the form. now we are confirming that we are holding it off.

I apologize for any confusion in my previous response. I understand that we previously informed the individual that we would wait for Nuvero's confirmation before processing the forms. I want to confirm that, after further discussion with Nuvero, we have collectively decided to hold off on the process of changing the owner of these accounts to the family trust. Consequently, the signed forms will be destroyed for compliance reasons. If you have any questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to contact Nuvero directly for clarification.

My pleasure, Ada & Paul. 

However, our accounting advised me that there’s one outstanding invoice in your file. This is for the meeting that happened last October (October 6, 2022) with Chris and Erica. I believe this meeting was to discuss the trust for Jenny and potential tax implications. Below is the link to the invoice and the payment can be made online by credit card.

Thank you for your prompt clarification, Ada & Paul.

I wanted to bring to your attention that our accounting department has informed me that there is an outstanding invoice in your file. This invoice pertains to the meeting that took place on October 6, 2022, with Chris and Erica regarding the trust for Jenny and the potential tax implications. 

To facilitate the payment process, I have included a link to the invoice below. You can conveniently make the payment online using a credit card.

[Link to the Invoice]

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions or require any further assistance.
I don’t think this is something that MEP should keep following up. Unless there are specific outstanding items that needs to be handled by MEP, we will close this task for Brooke.

Understood. If there are no specific outstanding items that require MEP's attention, we will close this task for Brooke. Please feel free to reach out if you have any further questions or if there is anything else MEP can assist with in the future.
please rephrase the first sentence

I understand that MEP should not continue to follow up on this matter unless there are any specific outstanding items that require our attention. In that case, we will be sure to address those promptly. As of now, we will proceed with closing this task for Brooke. If you have any further questions or if there is anything else MEP can assist with in the future, please don't hesitate to reach out.

I will attend in person on the 29th since Norman is off that day. Please obtain the Pension/LIF forms Robert is referring to in advance so that I can review. Sounds like a private pension package transfer but I will need to see the documents first. He's a CPA and CFP, interesting he's coming to us for planning. Please schedule an internal call as well before the meeting.

I will be present in person on the 29th since Norman will be off that day. Kindly ensure that the Pension/LIF forms Robert mentioned are obtained in advance, so that I can review them prior to the meeting. It appears to be a private pension package transfer, but I would like to examine the documents beforehand. It's intriguing that Robert, a CPA and CFP, is seeking our assistance for planning. In addition, please schedule an internal call before the meeting to discuss further details.
